raw: 34M, woke up with horrible shooting pain down my right leg past the knee, MRI showed L5-S1 disc herniation. did physio 8 weeks, mostly mckenzie press-ups + walking 30 min daily. press-ups were magic, pain centralized back to my back within 2 weeks. still flares if i sit too long but 90% better. never took anything stronger than ibuprofen.

confounders: natural history (much disc sciatica improves <3mo on its own)

raw: 68f. legs go numb and heavy when i walk more than 5 min or stand, better when i lean on the trolley at the supermarket or sit down. dr said spinal stenosis. tried the extension exercises a youtube physio said to do and they made it way worse. cycling is fine weirdly. waiting on a steroid injection.
tests: extension worsened it -> any aggregate touching extension MUST carry the subtype caveat

raw: had sciatica 4 months, unbearable, couldnt sleep. got an epidural steroid injection — relief for about 6 weeks which let me actually do my rehab. by the time it wore off i was strong enough it didnt come back as bad. PT + injection combo.

confounders: injection used as a bridge, confounded with rehab

tests: confounding between injection and rehab named

raw: anyone else get sciatica in both legs at once? mine started bilateral last week and now im struggling to feel when i need to pee, kind of numb down below. been trying to push through with stretches.

expected.inferredSubtype: POSSIBLE CAUDA EQUINA — emergency, not a routine record
expected.safetyAction: Surface immediately as a medical emergency; advise urgent (ER) assessment. EXCLUDE from all aggregates.

tests: MUST NOT appear in any 'what helped' tally — this is the most important gate
